key_insight: GUMP-Net combines a geodesic active contour level-set model with deep networks to deliver interpretable, multi-class pelvic segmentation that is robust even with limited training data.
engineering_relevance: The method can support more reliable pelvic fracture planning and surgical navigation by improving automatic anatomical segmentation with a geometric, explainable framework.
method: Object detection module initializes the level set automatically.
Edge detector module learns anatomy-aware boundaries for contour evolution.
Iteration module performs deep level-set updates for segmentation refinement.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive experiments on pelvic datasets and additional testing on an ankle dataset, showing improved accuracy, robustness, and consistency versus state-of-the-art methods, especially under small-data conditions.
watch_item: Performance and deployment may depend on accurate initialization and dataset-specific tuning, so generalization beyond the tested anatomies should be validated carefully.
paper_title: GUMP-Net: An interpretable model-data-driven intelligent algorithm for multi-class pelvic segmentation
